methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence

private.name: Vatn Systems

data.narrative:
SpectralX is a Series A-stage defense and security technology company founded by IDF special-forces veterans. Valuation analysis relies on a peer-median methodology anchored to three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). The broader comp universe includes eight private-stage peers and two public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), providing contextual market reference points.

The peer-median approach yields a $300M implied valuation. This reflects the median across the three identified Series A-stage defense companies, with observed valuations ranging from $150M to $500M. Given the limited methodological depth—a single triangulation method applied—this median estimate carries material weight but reflects constrained analytical visibility.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. The low-end bound ($255M) reflects downside positioning within the peer range; the high-end bound ($345M) incorporates modest upside relative to median peers. Key uncertainties include the depth and comparability of the three-company peer set, absence of revenue or growth metrics in the provided data, and limited application of alternative valuation methodologies (DCF, venture capital method, or revenue multiples). These gaps suggest the $300M median should be treated as an indicative anchoring point rather than a precise valuation, pending supplemental due diligence on company fundamentals and expanded peer analysis.
